# TEMA: Processo de Vendas High-Ticket

[[HOME|<- Home]] | [[MOC-TEMAS|Temas]] | [[MOC-PESSOAS|Pessoas]] | [[MOC-AGENTES|Agentes]]

> **Em resumo:** Filosofia > taticas. 7 Beliefs instaladas antes do pitch eliminam objecoes. Framework CLOSER (Hormozi) e 6-Phase Call Flow (Gordon) sao complementares. Conviction > Scripts. Prevencao de objecoes upstream, nao handling downstream. Speed to Lead e Pipeline estruturado sao multiplicadores de conversao.
>
> **Versao:** 4.0.0 | **Atualizado:** 2026-01-10
> **Densidade:** ALTA (5)
> **Fontes:** Alex Hormozi, Cole Gordon, Full Sales System, G4 Educacao, Jeremy Haynes

## Filosofia Central

O processo de vendas high-ticket combina estrutura (Call Flow) com filosofia (Beliefs). Ambos Hormozi e Gordon concordam: conviction supera scripts. A maestria vem de flexibilidade comportamental baseada em mental models corretos, nao de respostas decoradas.

```
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    PRINCIPIOS CONVERGENTES                                  |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                             |
|  HORMOZI: "I've never found a killer who doesn't close in first            |
|           2 weeks"                                                          |
|  -> Conviction e inata, scripts sao treinaveis                              |
|                                                                             |
|  GORDON: "If you have the right philosophy, tactics come naturally"         |
|  -> Mental models corretos geram adaptacao em tempo real                    |
|                                                                             |
|  AMBOS:                                                                     |
|  |-- Trust e construida no Discovery, nao no rapport                        |
|  |-- Internal pressure > external pressure                                  |
|  +-- Prevencao de objecoes > handling de objecoes                           |
|                                                                             |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Novas Filosofias Fundamentais [BATCH-128]

```
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    FILOSOFIAS ALEX HORMOZI                                  |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                             |
|  FIL-AH-016: "Nothing sells like the truth. The truth isn't just a good    |
|              strategy - it's the only long-term strategy."                  |
|                                                                             |
|  FIL-AH-019: "People believe almost nothing about what you say and         |
|              almost everything they say themselves."                        |
|                                                                             |
|  FIL-AH-026: "Whoever cares most about the prospect wins the sale.         |
|              You should care more about their outcome than they do."        |
|                                                                             |
|  FIL-AH-031: "The best salespeople never blame circumstances. The worst    |
|              say 'leads are bad.' Focus on things you can control."         |
|                                                                             |
|  FIL-AH-034: "People feel safer saying no. Saying yes makes them           |
|              vulnerable. Use no-based questions to get agreement."          |
|                                                                             |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Filosofias Pre-Vendas [BATCH-121]

```
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    FILOSOFIAS FULL SALES SYSTEM                             |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                             |
|  "Divisao pre-vendas/vendas (Aaron Ross) e o que grandes empresas           |
|   usam para ter comercial mais eficiente e previsivel"                      |
|                                                                             |
|  "Melhor ter pre-vendas bem estruturado que area de vendas -                |
|   e o coracao da empresa"                                                   |
|                                                                             |
|  "Agendamento e maior KPI - prefiro conversao baixa com muitos              |
|   agendamentos que alta conversao com poucos"                               |
|                                                                             |
|  "Quanto mais dificil cliente e de agendar, mais provavel e                 |
|   valioso ele e de comprar"                                                 |
|                                                                             |
|  "Lead se pega fresco, se pega quente - demora = probabilidade cai"         |
|                                                                             |
|  "SDR nao e atendentezinho - mostra personalidade, irreverencia,            |
|   nao e subordinado ao cliente"                                             |
|                                                                             |
+----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Modus Operandi

### Framework CLOSER (Hormozi)

Acronimo mnemonico para estrutura de call.

```
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
|   C   | Clarify why they're there - Por que agendou a call?       |
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
|   L   | Label it - Confirmar e nomear o objetivo                  |
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
|   O   | Overview past pain - Ciclo de dor, tentativas             |
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
|   S   | Sell the vacation - Vender destino, nao o voo             |
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
|   E   | Explain/handle concerns - Maximo 3 objecoes               |
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
|   R   | Reinforce decision - Video CS, proximos passos            |
+-------+-----------------------------------------------------------+
```

### 7 Beliefs Framework (Gordon)

Todo prospect precisa acreditar em 7 coisas para comprar. Qualquer objecao mapeia para uma delas.

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  AS 7 BELIEFS                                                                |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  INSTALADAS NO DISCOVERY (6):                                                |
|  +-----------+------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|  | 1. PAIN   | Ha problema OU desejo nao realizado                        |  |
|  | 2. DOUBT  | Acredita que e POSSIVEL resolver                           |  |
|  | 3. COST   | Custo da inacao > Custo do programa                        |  |
|  | 4. DESIRE | Quer o resultado especifico que voce oferece               |  |
|  | 5. MONEY  | Tem ou consegue recursos para investir                     |  |
|  | 6. SUPPORT| Tem apoio das pessoas relevantes                           |  |
|  +-----------+------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                              |
|  INSTALADA NO PITCH (1):                                                     |
|  +-----------+------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|  | 7. TRUST  | Confia que VOCE pode entregar o resultado                  |  |
|  +-----------+------------------------------------------------------------+  |
|                                                                              |
|  Objecao no final? Mapeie para uma das 7. Uma delas falhou.                  |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### 6-Phase Call Flow (Gordon)

```
+-- FASE 1: INTRODUCTION
|     |-- Rapport (apenas mostrar que voce e normal)
|     +-- Frame ("clean sheet of paper?")
|
+-- FASE 2: DISCOVERY
|     |-- Instalar 6 Beliefs via perguntas
|     |-- Usar Consistency Bias (prospect DIZ em voz alta)
|     |-- ATRIBUIR NUMEROS ("Last month exactly...")
|     +-- Hell Island vs Heaven Island (criar TENSAO)
|
+-- FASE 3: TRANSITION
|     |-- EVITAR Anvil Drop (transicao brusca)
|     +-- Fazer prospect PEDIR para ouvir o pitch
|
+-- FASE 4: PITCH (4 Pillars)
|     |-- High-Level Promise
|     |-- Pillar 1-2: Paradigm Shift
|     |-- Pillar 3-4: Future Pacing
|     |-- Delivery
|     +-- Check-in a cada 45 segundos (DOUBLE TIE DOWN)
|
+-- FASE 5: COMMITTING PHASE
|     |-- "What questions do you have SPECIFICALLY?"
|     |-- "Do you feel like this is what you've been looking for?"
|     +-- "Where do you want to go from here?"
|
+-- FASE 6: OBJECTION HANDLING (se necessario)
      +-- Se beliefs instalados corretamente, RARAMENTE chegamos aqui
```

## Speed to Lead [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    HEURISTICAS SPEED TO LEAD                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  <60 segundos resposta = 391% MAIOR probabilidade de fechar                  |
|  >5 minutos resposta = 80% QUEDA na taxa de fechamento                       |
|  50% dos prospects fecham com QUEM RESPONDE PRIMEIRO                         |
|  Same-day bookings tem MAIOR show rate que futuro                            |
|                                                                              |
|  REGRA: Primeiro contato em 2-5 minutos (automacao DevZap)                   |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## 5 Pilares da Area de Pre-Vendas [BATCH-121]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  FRAMEWORK: 5 PILARES DA AREA DE PRE-VENDAS                                  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|   PILAR 1: VENDER O AGENDAMENTO                                              |
|   ================================                                           |
|   * Vender oportunidade de falar com Closer                                  |
|   * Diagnostico gratuito, apresentacao do modelo                             |
|   * Personalizacao do contato                                                |
|   * Insistencia e persistencia criativa                                      |
|   * Gerar valor ao cliente                                                   |
|   * Tom de voz treinado                                                      |
|   * Ouvir o cliente (Dale Carnegie)                                          |
|   * Micro commitments no fechamento                                          |
|   * Paradoxo da escolha: 2 opcoes so                                         |
|                                                                              |
|   PILAR 2: FOLLOW-UP ESTRATEGICO                                             |
|   =================================                                          |
|   * Cadenciado, intenso e criativo                                           |
|   * 14 variacoes pre-setadas                                                 |
|   * +4 mensagens apos transferir                                             |
|   * Gestao por tags/estagios no CRM                                          |
|   * Mensagens pre-prontas                                                    |
|   * Blocos de tempo focados                                                  |
|   * Estado de flow                                                           |
|                                                                              |
|   PILAR 3: LIGACAO PARA AGENDAMENTO                                          |
|   ====================================                                       |
|   * Ligacao on time: 18h -> reuniao 18:30                                    |
|   * Role play diario 8:45 AM (20 min)                                        |
|   * Script de ligacao passo a passo                                          |
|   * Treinamento com simulacao                                                |
|                                                                              |
|   PILAR 4: REVERSAO DE RDOs                                                  |
|   ===========================                                                |
|   * R = Resposta por Reflexo ("nao to interessado")                          |
|   * D = Dispensa ("me liga mais tarde")                                      |
|   * O = Objecao ("nao creio que e pra mim")                                  |
|   * Matriz de RDO pre-definida                                               |
|   * Concordar para discordar                                                 |
|   * Compartilhar acertos -> virar padrao                                     |
|                                                                              |
|   PILAR 5: PROCESSO DE REMINDER                                              |
|   ================================                                           |
|   * Emails automaticos                                                       |
|   * SMS automaticos (2x)                                                     |
|   * WhatsApp manual (2-3x)                                                   |
|   * Ligacao de reminder no dia                                               |
|   * Hack: grupo WhatsApp (+20-30% comparecimento)                            |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Pipeline de Follow-up 6 Niveis [BATCH-120]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  FRAMEWORK: PIPELINE DE FOLLOW-UP 6 NIVEIS                                   |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|   ENTRADA    FU-1        FU-2         FU-3          FU-4        FU-5        |
|      |        |           |            |             |           |          |
|      V        V           V            V             V           V          |
|   +-----+  +--------+  +--------+  +----------+  +--------+  +---------+   |
|   |Lead |->| Nao    |->| Engajou|->|Interessado|->|No-Show |->|Repescagem|  |
|   |Novo |  |Respondeu|  |Conversa|  |em Agendar|  |3 tent. |  |Atendidos|   |
|   +-----+  +--------+  +--------+  +----------+  +--------+  +---------+   |
|               |                         |                          |        |
|               | 14 cadencias            | INFINITO                 | 15 dias|
|               | antes descarte          | Perda 0%                 | ultimato|
|               V                         V                          V        |
|          +---------+              +----------+               +---------+    |
|          |FU-4.5   |              |AGENDADO! |               |50% meta |    |
|          |Repesc.  |              |          |               |so aqui  |    |
|          |Mes      |              +----------+               |(V4)     |    |
|          |(SDR2)   |                                         +---------+    |
|          +---------+                                                        |
|               |                                                             |
|               V                                                             |
|          +---------+     +-------------+     +-------------+                |
|          |FU-6     | --> |SALES FARMING| --> |6 ciclos     | --> MARKETING  |
|          |Transfer |     |(SDR2)       |     |2 em 2 meses |                |
|          +---------+     +-------------+     +-------------+                |
|                                                                             |
|   RESULTADO: Perda reduziu de 70-75% para quase 0%                          |
|                                                                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

```
|                              ===============                                |
|                                   14:00h                                    |
|                             (cliente em rush)                               |

|                                                                              |
|   AGENDA B2C:                                                               |
|   * 4 atendimentos/dia por closer (maximo)                                  |
|   * 1h30 duracao por apresentacao com fechamento                            |
|   * 1h a 1h30 espacamento entre atendimentos                                |
|   * Time 1: 17h, 18:30h, 20h, 21:30h                                        |
|   * Time 2: 16:30h, 18h, 19:30h, 21h                                        |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## 5 C's of Sales Management [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    5 C'S OF SALES MANAGEMENT                                 |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  C1: CLOSER SEQUENCE                                                         |
|  |-- Use the CLOSER Framework (C-L-O-S-E-R)                                  |
|  |-- Everything must be questions, not statements                            |
|  +-- 30-second stories to illustrate each pillar                             |
|                                                                              |
|  C2: CALL RECORDINGS                                                         |
|  |-- Record EVERY call - no exceptions                                       |
|  |-- Makes team accountable to script                                        |
|  |-- Use Gong for Zoom calls                                                 |
|  +-- Weekly review: Sales + CS together                                      |
|                                                                              |
|  C3: COMMS (Communication Cadence)                                           |
|  |-- Daily huddles: share testimonials, remember why selling                 |
|  |-- Weekly one-on-ones                                                      |
|  |-- Tag 3 calls: best, worst, average                                       |
|  +-- Review where they clarified, outlined pain, asked for sale              |
|                                                                              |
|  C4: CUT                                                                     |
|  |-- Cut bottom 10% quarterly                                                |
|  |-- Creates 30% jump in productivity                                        |
|  +-- Even if team is doing okay - still cut                                  |
|                                                                              |
|  C5: COMPETITION                                                             |
|  |-- Leaderboard visible daily                                               |
|  |-- Bell (real or GIF) when sale closes                                     |
|  |-- 6-week competition cycles                                               |
|  |-- Three-man teams (root for each other)                                   |
|  +-- Prize: 25% of one month's check per person                              |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## No-Based Questions [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    NO-BASED QUESTIONS (Chris Voss)                           |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  THREE TYPES OF QUESTIONS:                                                   |
|  |-- Yes-based: "Sound great?" (harder to get)                               |
|  |-- Neutral: "Fair enough?" (easier)                                        |
|  +-- No-based: "Would you be opposed to...?" (easiest)                       |
|                                                                              |
|  WHY NO-BASED WORKS:                                                         |
|  |-- People feel safer saying no                                             |
|  |-- Retain sense of autonomy                                                |
|  |-- Less vulnerable than saying yes                                         |
|  +-- Especially powerful at end of day (decision fatigue)                    |
|                                                                              |
|  NO-BASED OPENERS:                                                           |
|  |-- "Would you be opposed to..."                                            |
|  |-- "Would it be completely unreasonable..."                                |
|  |-- "Would you be against..."                                               |
|  +-- "Would it be completely out of left field..."                           |
|                                                                              |
|  CLOSE APPLICATIONS:                                                         |
|  |-- "Would you be opposed to moving forward today?"                         |
|  |-- "Would you be opposed to knocking out the paperwork?"                   |
|  |-- "Would it be unreasonable for us to get started?"                       |
|  +-- No = Yes in disguise                                                    |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## Core Four Methods [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    CORE FOUR METHODS                                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  1. WARM OUTREACH                                                            |
|  |-- One-to-one to people you KNOW                                           |
|  |-- Gold in phone contacts, followers, past leads                           |
|  +-- Script: Intro -> "Do you have time for X?" -> "Know anyone else?"       |
|                                                                              |
|  2. COLD OUTREACH                                                            |
|  |-- One-to-one to people you DON'T know                                     |
|  |-- Three problems: List, Script, Volume                                    |
|  |-- Build/buy/scrape lists                                                  |
|  +-- Personalization + Big Fast Value                                        |
|                                                                              |
|  3. PAID ADS                                                                 |
|  |-- One-to-many to people you DON'T know                                    |
|  |-- Advertising is a BOOM (order of magnitude)                              |
|  +-- Hook is 80 cents of advertising dollar                                  |
|                                                                              |
|  4. CONTENT                                                                  |
|  |-- One-to-many to people you DO know                                       |
|  |-- Lubricant to other methods                                              |
|  |-- Short-form: top of funnel, polarizing hooks                             |
|  |-- Long-form: education, course-quality, trust                             |
|  +-- Pre-sell content: address objections                                    |
|                                                                              |
|  RULE: Pick ONE, go all in, first 4 hours every day                          |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## BAMFAM [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    BAMFAM (Book A Meeting From A Meeting)                    |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  RULE: Never finish call without knowing next touchpoint                     |
|                                                                              |
|  WHEN THEY STALL:                                                            |
|  |-- "Let's connect offline" -> NO! Schedule now                             |
|  |-- Calendars up, make decision together                                    |
|  |-- "I'm not sure" = address the real concern                               |
|  +-- Time obstacle is still an obstacle                                      |
|                                                                              |
|  QUESTIONS TO ASK:                                                           |
|  |-- "How much are you losing every day not solving this?"                   |
|  |-- "Why isn't this something we'd be doing sooner?"                        |
|  +-- "Isn't this a problem for you?"                                         |
|                                                                              |
|  OUTCOME: Prospect never falls into no man's land                            |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## Metodologias SPIN/BANT/VEFA [BATCH-104]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    METODOLOGIAS DE QUALIFICACAO                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  SPIN SELLING (Neil Rackham)                                                 |
|  |-- Situation: Entender contexto atual                                      |
|  |-- Problem: Identificar dores e desafios                                   |
|  |-- Implication: Consequencias de nao resolver                              |
|  +-- Need-Payoff: Valor da solucao                                           |
|      Pesquisa: 35.000 negociacoes analisadas                                 |
|                                                                              |
|  BANT                                                                        |
|  |-- Budget: Tem orcamento?                                                  |
|  |-- Authority: Tem poder de decisao?                                        |
|  |-- Need: Tem necessidade real?                                             |
|  +-- Timeline: Tem urgencia?                                                 |
|                                                                              |
|  VEFA (Foco em Valor)                                                        |
|  |-- Verificar situacao atual                                                |
|  |-- Explorar impacto do problema                                            |
|  |-- Focalizar na solucao                                                    |
|  +-- Assumir compromisso                                                     |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## Sistema de Reminder 5 Camadas [BATCH-121]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  FRAMEWORK: SISTEMA DE REMINDER (5 CAMADAS)                                  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|                        AGENDAMENTO FEITO                                     |
|                              |                                               |
|           +------------------+------------------+                            |
|           |                  |                  |                            |
|           V                  V                  V                            |
|   +---------------+  +---------------+  +---------------+                   |
|   | EMAILS AUTO   |  |  SMS AUTO     |  | WHATSAPP SDR  |                   |
|   |  (Sequencia)  |  |   (2x)        |  |   (2-3x)      |                   |
|   +---------------+  +---------------+  +---------------+                   |
|                              |                                               |
|                              V                                               |
|              +-------------------------------+                               |
|              |   LIGACAO REMINDER NO DIA     |                               |
|              |   (SDR liga para confirmar)   |                               |
|              +---------------+---------------+                               |
|                              |                                               |
|                              V                                               |
|        +---------------------------------------------+                       |
|        |   HACK: GRUPO WHATSAPP (+20-30%)            |                       |
|        |                                             |                       |
|        |   +-------------+                           |                       |
|        |   | Maria 19/02 |  <- Nome + Data + Horario |                       |
|        |   |   17:00h    |                           |                       |
|        |   +-------------+                           |                       |
|        |                                             |                       |
|        |   Participantes: Cliente + SDR + Closer     |                       |
|        |   Pedir confirmacao NO GRUPO                |                       |
|        |   Gatilho: compromisso publico (Cialdini)   |                       |
|        |                                             |                       |
|        +---------------------------------------------+                       |
|                                                                              |
|   RESULTADO: 60% -> 66% -> 90% (B2B)                                         |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Arsenal Tecnico

### Tonalidade - 3 Niveis (Gordon)

| Nivel | Uso | Caracteristicas |
|-------|-----|-----------------|
| Seeking Rapport | EVITAR | Inflexao ascendente, eyebrows up |
| Neutral | 80-90% da call | Tom relaxado, confiante |
| Breaking Rapport | 5-10% | Inflexao descendente, perguntas dificeis |

### Double Tie Down (Gordon)

```
"Does that make sense?"
    +
"What are your thoughts on that?"

Usar a cada ~45 segundos no pitch
Transforma monologo em dialogo
```

### Atribuicao de Numeros (Gordon)

```
ERRADO: "How's business going?"
CERTO: "What did you do last month, exactly?"

Pessoas se identificam com melhor mes, nao com media
"Last month EXACTLY" expoe realidade
```

---

## Heuristicas Quantificadas [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    HEURISTICAS COM NUMEROS                                   |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  SPEED TO LEAD                                                               |
|  |-- <60 seconds response = 391% higher close likelihood                     |
|  |-- >5 minutes response = 80% drop in close rate                            |
|  |-- 50% of prospects go with whoever responds FIRST                         |
|  +-- Same-day bookings have HIGHER show rates than future                    |
|                                                                              |
|  WEEKLY AVAILABILITY                                                         |
|  |-- 7 days/week selling = +29% revenue                                      |
|  |-- 52 weeks x 2 weekend days = 104 extra selling days/year                 |
|  +-- Businesses pay rent on Sundays too                                      |
|                                                                              |
|  WORD OF MOUTH (Disney Study)                                                |
|  |-- 37 magic moments to make up for 1 tragic moment                         |
|  |-- Good experience: tell 5 people                                          |
|  |-- Bad experience: tell EVERYONE (5x vs 37x)                               |
|  +-- Negative word of mouth significantly stronger                           |
|                                                                              |
|  REFERRAL IMPACT                                                             |
|  |-- 1 in 3 customers refers someone                                         |
|  |-- Referral close rate: 80-90%                                             |
|  |-- One referrer per 4 closes = 2 sales out of 5                            |
|  +-- Cuts CAC by 33-50%                                                      |
|                                                                              |
|  70/20/10 RULE (Google/Page/Brin)                                            |
|  |-- 70% = exact carbon copy of what works                                   |
|  |-- 20% = adjacent variations                                               |
|  |-- 10% = brand new experiments                                             |
|  +-- Most entrepreneurs flip this (70% new, 10% proven)                      |
|                                                                              |
|  PREPARATION METRICS                                                         |
|  |-- 5 minutes research = sound like genius                                  |
|  |-- 10% of call time = appropriate prep time                                |
|  |-- 60-min call = 6 min prep                                                |
|  +-- Quarterly meeting: reverses to 6 hours prep for 1 hour                  |
|                                                                              |
|  SALES TEAM PRODUCTIVITY                                                     |
|  |-- Cut bottom 10% quarterly = 30% jump in productivity                     |
|  |-- 6 weeks = optimal competition cycle                                     |
|  |-- 25% of one month's check = competition prize budget                     |
|  |-- Three-man teams for competitions (share best practices)                 |
|  +-- Best salespeople listen 2x as much as they talk                         |
|                                                                              |
|  HOOK IMPACT                                                                 |
|  |-- Snipped 3 seconds from hook: 40K -> 780K views (19x)                    |
|  |-- Hook = 80 cents of advertising dollar (Ogilvy)                          |
|  |-- Different intro for same 60-min talk = different NPS score              |
|  +-- Same words, different emphasis = different meaning                      |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Heuristicas Pre-Vendas [BATCH-121]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  HEURISTICAS QUANTIFICADAS - PRE-VENDAS                                      |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  CALENDARIOS                                                                 |
|  * 6 calendarios = 1 por closer na empresa                                   |
|  * +10 closers: usar Collective Booking (mesmo calendario)                   |
|  * 30 minutos intervalo entre reunioes                                       |
|  * 30 minutos duracao reuniao                                                |
|  * 1 hora total entre reuniao e proxima                                      |
|  * Horarios fixados: 9:30, 11:00, 14:00, 15:30, 17:00, 18:30                 |
|                                                                              |
|  PRE-VENDAS (5 PILARES)                                                      |
|  * 3-5 agendamentos/dia por SDR (meta ideal)                                 |
|  * 50% conversao: SDR de alta performance                                    |
|  * 15-20% conversao: SDR medio                                               |
|  * 14 variacoes de follow-up (WhatsApp + ligacao)                            |
|  * +4 mensagens apos "desistir" do lead                                      |
|  * 5 minutos: tempo maximo para primeiro contato (funil digital)             |
|  * 20 minutos: tempo para contato em aplicacoes                              |
|  * 120 clientes: capacidade follow-up em 1 hora focada                       |
|  * 8:45 AM: horario role play diario                                         |
|  * 20 minutos: duracao treinamento diario                                    |
|  * 60% no show: taxa SEM acoes de reminder                                   |
|  * 60-66%: comparecimento COM acoes                                          |
|  * 90%: comparecimento em alguns negocios B2B                                |
|  * 20-30% aumento: resultado hack grupo WhatsApp                             |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Empilhamento de Funis [BATCH-117]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  FRAMEWORK: EMPILHAMENTO DE FUNIS - 3 DENSIDADES                             |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  +-----------------+                                                         |
|  | ALTA DENSIDADE  |  Funil de Aplicacao                                     |
|  |   (Barreira     |  -> Lead ja quer comprar                                |
|  |    Alta)        |  -> 25% taxa de agendamento                             |
|  |                 |  -> R$12,50-25/lead                                     |
|  +--------+--------+                                                         |
|           |                                                                  |
|  +--------v--------+                                                         |
|  | MEDIA DENSIDADE |  Funil de Webinar                                       |
|  |   (Barreira     |  -> Consciencia elevada                                 |
|  |    Media)       |  -> Comercial contacta todos                            |
|  |                 |  -> Pode jogar para aplicacao                           |
|  +--------+--------+                                                         |
|           |                                                                  |
|  +--------v--------+                                                         |
|  | BAIXA DENSIDADE |  Funil de Ebook / Isca Gratuita                         |
|  |   (Barreira     |  -> Facil conversao                                     |
|  |    Baixa)       |  -> 10,35% taxa agendamento                             |
|  |                 |  -> R$0,83-4,93/lead                                    |
|  +-----------------+                                                         |
|                                                                              |
|  SOCIAL SELLING (Pre-Funil):                                                 |
|  * Seguidor -> Conversa -> Agendamento -> Venda                              |
|  * R$1/seguidor -> R$30K+ em vendas                                          |
|  * Nao precisa cadastro formal em funil                                      |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Catalogo de Closes [AH-BP001]

O $100M Closing Playbook documenta 40+ tecnicas de fechamento. Resultado: $100k -> $1.5M/mes em 12 meses via treinamento de objecoes.

### Closes Universais (Uso em Qualquer Situacao)

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  CLOSES ALL-PURPOSE                                                          |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  HYPOTHETICAL CLOSE                                                          |
|  +-- "Hipoteticamente, se resolvessemos X, voce estaria pronto?"             |
|      Contorna resistencia sem pressao direta                                 |
|                                                                              |
|  ZOOM OUT CLOSE                                                              |
|  +-- "Se voce olhar sua vida daqui 5 anos..."                                |
|      Forca perspectiva de longo prazo                                        |
|                                                                              |
|  1 TO 10 CLOSE                                                               |
|  +-- "De 1 a 10, quao comprometido voce esta?"                               |
|      Qualquer resposta <10 = descobrir o gap                                 |
|                                                                              |
|  MAIN CONCERN CLOSE                                                          |
|  +-- "Qual sua principal preocupacao?"                                       |
|      Isolar a UNICA objecao real                                             |
|                                                                              |
|  BEST CASE / WORST CASE [BATCH-128]                                          |
|  +-- "Qual o melhor cenario? E o pior?"                                      |
|      Worst case: same position. Best case: life changed.                     |
|      "Does that feel like a bet you're willing to take?"                     |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

### Closes Situacionais (Por Tipo de Objecao)

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  CLOSES POR SITUACAO                                                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  OBJECAO DE PRECO:                                                           |
|  |-- FOUR MONEY FLAVORS -> 4 formas de conseguir dinheiro                    |
|  |-- IT'S GOOD THAT IT'S A LOT -> Preco alto = filtro de comprometimento     |
|  +-- SPEND MONEY EITHER WAY -> Vai gastar de qualquer forma                  |
|                                                                              |
|  OBJECAO DE TEMPO:                                                           |
|  |-- WHEN/THEN TRAP -> "Quando X, entao Y" e armadilha de procrastinacao     |
|  +-- ANOTHER YEAR OF ALMOST -> "Mais um ano de quase conseguir?"             |
|                                                                              |
|  PRECISO CONSULTAR ALGUEM:                                                   |
|  +-- DECISION MAKER SEQUENCE -> Protocolo para incluir conjuge/socio         |
|                                                                              |
|  CARTAO NAO ESTA COMIGO:                                                     |
|  +-- SMARTPHONE CLOSE -> "Voce tem app do banco no celular?"                 |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## Armadilhas

### Anvil Drop

Transicao brusca do discovery pro pitch. O prospect sente que voce estava so esperando a chance de vender. Em vez disso, fazer o prospect PEDIR para ouvir.

### Trust no Rapport

Tentar construir trust no rapport inicial e perda de tempo. Rapport serve apenas para mostrar que voce e humano normal. TRUST e construida no Discovery ao demonstrar que entende profundamente o problema.

### Fast Action Discounts

Desconto por urgencia artificial atrai clientes que compram por pressao externa. Esses sao os que cancelam. Internal pressure (razoes do proprio prospect) cria vendas duraveis.

### Handling em vez de Prevencao

Se voce esta tratando objecao no final da call, voce ja perdeu. Prevencao de objecoes acontece upstream - no discovery, instalando as 7 beliefs.

### Kill Zombies Up Front [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  MODELO MENTAL: KILL ZOMBIES UP FRONT                                        |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  |-- Obstacles = BEFORE price, easy to handle                                |
|  |-- Objections = AFTER price, bomb is ticking                               |
|  +-- Handle spouse/time/money BEFORE you mention price                       |
|                                                                              |
|  REGRA: Descobrir todas as objecoes ANTES de revelar preco                   |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## Convergencias Hormozi + Gordon

| Principio | Hormozi (CLOSER) | Gordon (7 Beliefs) |
|-----------|------------------|-------------------|
| Conviction > Scripts | "Killers close in 2 weeks" | "Philosophy > tactics" |
| Internal > External | Implicito (foco em valor) | Explicito ("training wheels") |
| Trust no Discovery | "Overview past pain" | "Trust built in discovery" |
| Prevencao > Handling | "E" = maximo 3 objecoes | "Prevention is upstream" |

**Tipo de tensao:** COMPLEMENTAR - Hormozi da estrutura, Gordon da filosofia.

## Metricas de Processo (Hormozi)

| Fase | Metrica | Benchmark |
|------|---------|-----------|
| BDR | Calls/dia | 100 |
| BDR | Pickup rate | 20% |
| BDR | Shows agendados | 2/dia |
| Close rate (antes) | - | 20-25% |
| Close rate (depois) | - | 60% |
| Identificar "killer" | Timeline | 2 semanas |

## 9 Things Brilliant Salespeople Do [BATCH-128]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                    9 THINGS BRILLIANT SALESPEOPLE DO                         |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|  THREE JOBS OF A SALESPERSON:                                                |
|  |-- 1. Maximize opportunities                                               |
|  |-- 2. Convert highest percentage                                           |
|  +-- 3. Do it consistently for a long time                                   |
|                                                                              |
|  BUCKET 1 - MAXIMIZE OPPORTUNITIES:                                          |
|  |-- 1. Maximum hours/days available                                         |
|  |-- 2. Pull up appointments (today > Thursday)                              |
|  |-- 3. Don't take rejection personally                                      |
|  |-- 4. Keep a "kill list" (high-value visible)                              |
|  +-- 5. Create own opportunities (referrals)                                 |
|                                                                              |
|  BUCKET 2 - CONVERT HIGHER PERCENTAGE:                                       |
|  |-- 6. Understand value of preparation (5 min research)                     |
|  |-- 7. Listen more than talk (2:1 ratio)                                    |
|  |-- 8. Breathe the script (say without thinking)                            |
|  +-- 9. Kill zombies up front (obstacles before price)                       |
|                                                                              |
|  BUCKET 3 - CONSISTENCY:                                                     |
|  |-- Maintain enthusiasm as a skill                                          |
|  |-- Kill for sport (tigers, not dogs)                                       |
|  |-- Track data meticulously                                                 |
|  +-- Never blame circumstances                                               |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

---

## Gestao de Tempo do SDR [BATCH-121]

```
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  FRAMEWORK: GESTAO DE TEMPO DO SDR (BLOCOS)                                  |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                              |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  08:45 - 09:05  |  ROLE PLAY DIARIO (20 min)                       |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  09:05 - 10:00  |  CONTATO LEADS NOVOS + LIGACAO REMINDER          |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  10:00 - 11:00  |  FOLLOW-UP NIVEL 1 (nao responderam)             |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  11:00 - 12:00  |  FOLLOW-UP NIVEL 2 (engajaram)                   |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  13:00 - 14:00  |  FOLLOW-UP NIVEL 3 (interessados)                |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  14:00 - 15:00  |  FOLLOW-UP NO-SHOW (3 tentativas)                |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  15:00 - 16:00  |  AGENDAMENTO ON TIME (ligacao -> call imediata)  |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  16:00 - 17:00  |  NOVOS AGENDAMENTOS                              |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|   |  17:00 - 18:00  |  FOLLOW-UP REPESCAGEM (final do mes)             |    |
|   +--------------------------------------------------------------------+    |
|                                                                              |
|   PRINCIPIO: Uma tarefa por bloco -> Estado de FLOW -> Sem gasto mental     |
|                                                                              |
+-----------------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Citacoes Originais

> "If you have the right philosophy, the tactics and scripts come naturally."
> -- Cole Gordon [CG001]

> "Objection prevention is upstream, objection handling is downstream."
> -- Cole Gordon [CG001]

> "I've never found a killer who doesn't close in the first 2 weeks."
> -- Alex Hormozi [SS001]

> "Nothing sells like the truth. The truth isn't just a good strategy - it's the only long-term strategy."
> -- Alex Hormozi [BATCH-128]

> "People feel safer saying no. Saying yes makes them vulnerable."
> -- Alex Hormozi [BATCH-128]

> "Divisao pre-vendas/vendas (Aaron Ross) e o que grandes empresas usam para ter comercial mais eficiente e previsivel."
> -- Full Sales System [BATCH-121]
